speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Great lords, wise men ne’er sit and wail their loss

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But cheerly seek how to redress their harms.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What though the mast be now blown overboard,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The cable broke, the holding-anchor lost,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And half our sailors swallowed in the flood?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Yet lives our pilot still. Is ’t meet that he

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Should leave the helm and, like a fearful lad,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: With tearful eyes add water to the sea

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And give more strength to that which hath too much,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Whiles in his moan the ship splits on the rock,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Which industry and courage might have saved?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Ah, what a shame, ah, what a fault were this!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Say Warwick was our anchor; what of that?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And Montague our topmast; what of him?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Our slaughtered friends the tackles; what of these?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why, is not Oxford here another anchor?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And Somerset another goodly mast?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: The friends of France our shrouds and tacklings?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And, though unskillful, why not Ned and I

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: For once allowed the skillful pilot’s charge?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: We will not from the helm to sit and weep,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: But keep our course, though the rough wind say no,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: From shelves and rocks that threaten us with wrack.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: As good to chide the waves as speak them fair.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And what is Edward but a ruthless sea?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: What Clarence but a quicksand of deceit?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And Richard but a ragged fatal rock—

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: All these the enemies to our poor bark?

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Say you can swim: alas, ’tis but awhile;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Tread on the sand: why, there you quickly sink;

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Bestride the rock: the tide will wash you off

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Or else you famish; that’s a threefold death.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This speak I, lords, to let you understand,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: If case some one of you would fly from us,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: That there’s no hoped-for mercy with the brothers

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: More than with ruthless waves, with sands and rocks.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Why, courage then! What cannot be avoided

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: ’Twere childish weakness to lament or fear.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Methinks a woman of this valiant spirit

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Should, if a coward heard her speak these words,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Infuse his breast with magnanimity

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And make him, naked, foil a man-at-arms.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: I speak not this as doubting any here,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: For did I but suspect a fearful man,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: He should have leave to go away betimes,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Lest in our need he might infect another

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And make him of like spirit to himself.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: If any such be here, as God forbid,

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: Let him depart before we need his help.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Women and children of so high a courage,

speaker: OXFORD
text: And warriors faint? Why, ’twere perpetual shame!

speaker: OXFORD
text: O, brave young prince, thy famous grandfather

speaker: OXFORD
text: Doth live again in thee. Long mayst thou live

speaker: OXFORD
text: To bear his image and renew his glories!

speaker: SOMERSET
text: And he that will not fight for such a hope,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: Go home to bed and, like the owl by day,

speaker: SOMERSET
text: If he arise, be mocked and wondered at.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Thanks, gentle Somerset.—Sweet Oxford, thanks.

speaker:
PRINCE
               
              EDWARD
text: And take his thanks that yet hath nothing else.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Prepare you, lords, for Edward is at hand,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Ready to fight. Therefore be resolute.

speaker: OXFORD
text: I thought no less. It is his policy

speaker: OXFORD
text: To haste thus fast to find us unprovided.

speaker: SOMERSET
text: But he’s deceived. We are in readiness.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: This cheers my heart to see your forwardness.

speaker: OXFORD
text: Here pitch our battle; hence we will not budge.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Brave followers, yonder stands the thorny wood

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Which by the heavens’ assistance and your strength

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Must by the roots be hewn up yet ere night.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: I need not add more fuel to your fire,

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: For, well I wot, you blaze to burn them out.

speaker:
KING
               
              EDWARD
text: Give signal to the fight, and to it, lords!

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Lords, knights, and gentlemen, what I should say

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: My tears gainsay, for every word I speak

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: You see I drink the water of my eye.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Therefore, no more but this: Henry, your sovereign,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Is prisoner to the foe, his state usurped,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His realm a slaughterhouse, his subjects slain,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: His statutes cancelled and his treasure spent,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: And yonder is the wolf that makes this spoil.

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: You fight in justice. Then, in God’s name, lords,

speaker:
QUEEN
               
              MARGARET
text: Be valiant, and give signal to the fight!
